2605.18530.title: Continuous Diffusion Scales Competitively with Discrete Diffusion for Language
2605.18530.paper_summary: While diffusion has drawn considerable recent attention from the language modeling community, continuous diffusion has appeared less scalable than discrete approaches. To challenge this belief we revisit Plaid, a likelihood-based continuous diffusion language model (DLM), and construct RePlaid by aligning the architecture of Plaid with modern discrete DLMs. In this unified setting, we establish the first scaling law for continuous DLMs that rivals discrete DLMs: RePlaid exhibits a compute gap of only $20\times$ compared to autoregressive models, outperforms Duo while using fewer parameters, and outperforms MDLM in the over-trained regime. We benchmark RePlaid against recent continuous DLMs: on OpenWebText, RePlaid achieves a new state-of-the-art PPL bound of $22.1$ among continuous DLMs and superior generation quality. These results suggest that continuous diffusion, when trained via likelihood, is a highly competitive and scalable alternative to discrete DLMs. Moreover, we offer theoretical insights to understand the advantage of likelihood-based training. We show that optimizing the noise schedule to minimize the ELBO's variance naturally yields linear cross-entropy (information loss) over time. This evenly distributes denoising difficulty without any case-specific time reparameterization. In addition, we find that optimizing embeddings via likelihood creates structured geometries and drives the most significant likelihood gain.
2605.18530.paper_summary_zh: 雖然擴散最近引起了語言建模社群的相當關注，但連續擴散似乎不如離散方法可擴展。為了挑戰這一信念，我們重新檢視了Plaid，一種基於似然的連續擴散語言模型（DLM），並通過將Plaid的架構與現代離散DLM對齊來構建RePlaid。在這個統一的設定中，我們建立了連續DLM的首個擴展法則，與離散DLM相媲美：RePlaid相比自回歸模型僅顯示出$20\times$的計算差距，在使用更少參數的情況下超越Duo，並在過度訓練的範疇中超越MDLM。我們將RePlaid與最近的連續DLM進行基準測試：在OpenWebText上，RePlaid在連續DLM中達到了新的最先進PPL界限$22.1$，並且生成質量優越。這些結果表明，當通過似然訓練時，連續擴散是一種高度競爭且可擴展的替代方案，相較於離散DLM。此外，我們提供了理論見解來理解基於似然訓練的優勢。我們顯示，優化噪聲時間表以最小化ELBO的方差自然隨時間產生線性交叉熵（信息損失）。這均勻分配了去噪的難度，而不需要任何特定案例的時間重新參數化。此外，我們發現通過似然優化嵌入會創造結構化幾何，並驅動最顯著的似然增益。
2605.18530.author: Zhihan Yang et.al.
2605.18530.authors: Zhihan Yang, Wei Guo, Shuibai Zhang, Subham Sekhar Sahoo, Yongxin Chen, Arash Vahdat, Morteza Mardani, John Thickstun
